channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Effects of physical exercise on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis: A systematic review.
item.description: A systematic review analyzed the effectiveness of different physical exercise interventions on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis, including randomized controlled trials with EDSS scores ≤6.0.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Follow up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on paediatric ACL injuries: towards a broader vision of care
item.description: A follow-up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on pediatric ACL injuries provides an updated vision of care, examining recent advances, current evidence, and persistent challenges in prevention, individualized management, evolving surgical strategies, and long-term holistic care.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating in athletes: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis estimated the prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating among athletes across various training levels and assessment methods, identifying moderators associated with these prevalence estimates.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: This RCT (n=140) found that a 6-week cardiovascular rehabilitation program significantly improved functional exercise capacity (6MWTD, moderate effect size) and physical activity levels over 6 months in TIA and mild stroke patients compared to usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study found that an advanced orthopedic virtual physical therapy model achieved a 70.9% care episode completion rate (n=61/86), exceeding the national benchmark of 43%, and 96% of completers achieved Minimal Clinically Important Differences.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials found pelvic floor muscle training to be effective for pelvic organ prolapse.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: APTA Report Finds Demand for Pelvic Health Physical Therapy Outpaces Access
item.description: A new APTA report, "APTA State of Pelvic Health Physical Therapy," reveals that demand for pelvic health physical therapy in the U.S. significantly outpaces access, highlighting workforce, access, and awareness challenges.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia: a systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis | Journal of NeuroEngineering and Rehabilitation
item.description: A systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis assessed the effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking in inpatient stroke rehabilitation: a multicentre cohort study.
item.description: A multicenter retrospective cohort study of 565 stroke patients determined the independent predictive value of admission Berg Balance Scale scores for independent walking at discharge and derived subtype-specific optimal cut-off values for ischemic and hemorrhagic stroke.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Incidence of injury and illness at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games: post-pandemic trends and sport-specific risks
item.description: A prospective surveillance study at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games monitored 4450 athletes, reporting 535 injuries (prevalence 10.9%) with an incidence of 8.6 per 1000 athlete days, identifying taekwondo (27.3) and football 5-a-side (22.3) as high-risk sports.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: GaitEncoder: A Foundation Model of Gait Kinematics for Diverse Clinical Applications and Pathologies
item.description: GaitEncoder is a generative foundation model of walking kinematics that integrates eight gait datasets (65,000+ recordings) to enable various downstream clinical tasks across diverse patient populations using smartphone video-based gait analysis.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: The psychosocial impacts of having eating and drinking difficulties after stroke: a qualitative evidence synthesis.
item.description: This systematic qualitative evidence synthesis (22 studies, 645 informants) explored the psychosocial impacts of eating and drinking difficulties after stroke from the perspectives of stroke survivors, informal carers, and healthcare professionals, identifying significant psychological and social domains.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: This retrospective cohort study of 333 ACDF patients found that sarcopenia significantly increases the risk of radiographic adjacent segment degeneration within 3 years post-surgery.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes.
item.description: This quasi-experimental study (n=72) found that a 12-week exergaming intervention (Nintendo Switch Ring Fit Adventure) significantly improved physical, psychological, and social outcomes in institutionalized older adults with sarcopenia compared to a control group.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effects of exercise on physical function and pain in adults with rheumatoid arthritis: an exit meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
item.description: An exit meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials found moderate certainty evidence that exercise, compared to no exercise, improved physical function and pain in adults with rheumatoid arthritis.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: This systematic review and meta-analysis examined the impact of postpartum exercise on pelvic floor disorders…
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis examined the impact of postpartum exercise on pelvic floor disorders and diastasis recti abdominis.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Immediate Weight-Bearing and Early Ambulation Rehabilitation After Femoral Neck System Fixation for Undisplaced Femoral Neck Fractures: A Prospective Randomized Controlled Trial
item.description: This prospective randomized controlled trial investigated immediate weight-bearing and early ambulation rehabilitation after femoral neck system fixation for undisplaced femoral neck fractures.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Frailty assessment and effect of physical activity interventions in older adults: a scoping review, meta-analysis, and meta-regression
item.description: A scoping review, meta-analysis, and meta-regression assessed frailty assessment and the effect of physical activity interventions in older adults.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Exploration of lifestyle interventions for individuals with knee osteoarthritis: A scoping review.
item.description: A scoping review identified 36 studies (29 RCTs) on lifestyle interventions for knee osteoarthritis, exploring their range, characteristics, and outcomes, as well as the assessment measures used.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Physical therapy protocols for patients with a megaprosthesis after bone tumor resection.
item.description: Physical therapy protocols for patients with megaprostheses after bone tumor resection were developed through an explorative literature search and consensus procedures involving orthopedic oncologic surgeons and physical therapists, focusing on six different megaprostheses.
item.author: PubMed
